summary: Iteration 37: Double-domain URL bug fix verified. All 14 upload handlers in 10 admin components now correctly check if URL starts with 'http' before prepending API base URL. Backend upload endpoints return Cloudinary URLs correctly. Frontend pages /sauny and /balie load without double-domain issues.
critical_code_review_comments: All 10 admin components correctly implement the fix: `data.url.startsWith('http') ? data.url : ${API}${data.url}`

seed_data_creation: Created test images via POST /api/admin/upload during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Double-domain URL bug is fixed. The bug was: when uploading media via admin panel, URLs were formed as 'https://domain.comhttps://res.cloudinary.com/...' because handlers concatenated API base URL with Cloudinary URL without checking if URL was already absolute. Fix: all 14 upload handlers now check `data.url.startsWith('http')` before prepending API base URL. Cloudinary integration is fully working.

backend_image_upload_cloudinary_url.details: POST /api/admin/upload returns Cloudinary URL starting with https://res.cloudinary.com/

backend_url_format_no_double_domain.details: Returned URLs don't contain 'hosthttps' or other double-domain patterns

frontend_sauny_page.details: /sauny loads without errors, 17 Cloudinary URLs, no double-domain issues

frontend_balie_page.details: /balie loads without errors, 25 Cloudinary URLs, 1 Cloudinary video, no double-domain issues

frontend_resolveMediaUrl.details: resolveMediaUrl passes Cloudinary URLs unchanged (verified in utils.js code)

admin_upload_handlers_fixed.details: All 14 upload handlers in 10 admin components use data.url.startsWith('http') check
